title: Scaffolder update: stop false-positive drift when a file matches the incoming template
description: `to-execution update` classifies an invariant file as drifted whenever its current hash differs from the recorded stamped-form hash, even when the file is byte-identical to the incoming template (src/bin/cli.js:658 checks current != recorded before comparing current to templateHash). The stale anchor is then carried forward (cli.js:663), so the false positive repeats on every future update. Dogfood test: 8 of 9 reported-drifted files were identical to the template. Fix: when currentHash === templateHash, classify the file unchanged and record templateHash, before the drift check. End-to-end: run update on an Instance whose file lockstep-matches the new template — it reports unchanged, and the rewritten marker anchors the template hash.
acceptance_criteria: A file byte-identical to the incoming template is reported unchanged, never drifted, regardless of the recorded hash
After update, the version marker records the template hash for such files (stale anchor not carried forward)
A file that genuinely differs from both the recorded hash and the template is still reported drifted and left in place
package-qa and code-standards gates PASS on the change
Dogfood root re-run of update reports the 8 previously false-positive files as unchanged
root_cause: update() orders the recorded-hash drift check before the template-content comparison, so lockstep-updated files with a stale anchor are misclassified
fix: update() reordered to test currentHash === templateHash before the recorded-hash drift check: a file byte-identical to the incoming template is classified unchanged and re-anchored to the template hash. Commit 93f1489 (src/bin/cli.js). code-standards + package-qa PASS.
notes: Found dogfood-testing the upgrade path (2026-06-10). The one genuine drift in that test (_teammate.template.md missing the color line) is being caught up directly.
